NUCLEAR SAFETY ANALYSIS:   PROVIDE ASSISTANCE AND ADVISORY SERVICES RELATED TO SAFETY REVIEWS AND ANALYSIS, TO ENVIRONMENTAL SAFETY AND BIOLOGICAL RISK ASSESSMENTS, ANALYSIS OF HEAT SOURCE IMPACT, THERMAL, AND STRUCTURAL MODELING, AND TO LAUNCH METEOROLOGICAL ANALYSIS USED IN THE PREPARATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENTS AND SAFETY ANALYSIS REPORTS.

THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE (NPS) SEEKS TO COMPLETE AN ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT (EIS) TO ANALYZE A PROPOSAL AND A RANGE OF ALTERNATIVES FOR CONSTRUCTING A HIGH VOLTAGE ELECTRIC TRANSMISSION LINE AFFECTING FOUR NPS UNITS AND A NATIONAL FOREST IN MARYLAND, WEST VIRGINIA AND VIRGINIA. THESE FEDERAL LANDS INCLUDE CHESAPEAKE AND OHIO CANAL NATIONAL HISTORICAL PARK (CHOH), HARPERS FERRY NATIONAL HISTORICAL PARK (HAFE), APPALACHIAN NATIONAL SCENIC TRAIL (APPA), POTOMAC HERITAGE NATIONAL SCENIC TRAIL (PNST) AND MONONGAHELA NATIONAL FOREST (MNF) (SEE MAP ON PAGE 4). THE CONTRACTOR SHALL COMPLETE PHASE 1 OF A THREE PHASE PROJECT BY BEGINNING PREPARATION OF A DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT (EIS) FOR THE POTOMAC-APPALACHIAN TRANSMISSION HIGHLINE (PATH).  PHASE 1 SHALL INCLUDE (1) INTERNAL SCOPING, (2) PROJECT SCHEDULE AND STATUS CALLS, (3) ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, (4) TECHNICAL AND COMPLIANCE REVIEW OF PROPOSAL, (5) DATA COLLECTION FOR ENVIRONMENTAL ANALYSIS AND FOR DEVELOPING THE ALTERNATIVES, (6) PUBLIC SCOPING, AND (7) PUBLIC COMMENT ANALYSIS.  THE SECOND PHASE MAY BE AWARDED AS ADDITIONAL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE. THE SECOND PHASE INCLUDES PREPARATION AND FACILITATION OF DEVELOPMENT OF ALTERNATIVES, INCLUDING (1) ANY ADDITIONAL FIELD SURVEYS TO FILL DATA GAPS NOT CONDUCTED IN PHASE I, (2) CONCEPTUAL PLANS THAT DESCRIBE AND ILLUSTRATE THE ALTERNATIVES, (3) COST ESTIMATES FOR EACH ALTERNATIVE, (4) ALTERNATIVES MEETING PREPARATION, (5) PUBLIC ALTERNATIVES WORKSHOPS, (6) SUMMARY OF PUBLIC COMMENT ON ALTERNATIVES, (7) A VALUE ANALYSIS PROCESS FOR IDENTIFYING THE PREFERRED ALTERNATIVE, (8) CONTINUE TO MAINTAIN THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, AND (9) CONTINUE MONTHLY STATUS CALLS. OPTIONS FOR DEVELOPING THE ALTERNATIVES UNDER PHASE 2 ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THIS AWARD OF THE DELIVERY ORDER, BUT MAY BE ADDED VIA MODIFICATION TO THIS DELIVERY ORDER OR A SEPARATE DELIVERY ORDER AT A LATER DATE.  THE THIRD PHASE MAY ALSO BE AWARDED AS ADDITIONAL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE. THE THIRD PHASE INCLUDES COMPLETING THE EIS AND ASSOCIATED DOCUMENTS. UNDER THE THIRD PHASE THE CONTRACTOR WOULD COMPLETE: (1) TWO INTERNAL DRAFT EISS WITH  REVISIONS AND REPRINTING AFTER EACH REVIEW, (2) OTHER REQUIRED ENVIRONMENTAL AND REGULATORY DOCUMENTS, (3) A CAMERA READY DRAFT EIS FOR PUBLIC REVIEW, (4) PUBLIC REVIEW OF THE DRAFT EIS, (5) ANALYSIS OF ALL COMMENTS ON THE DRAFT EIS, (6) A FINAL EIS, (7) NOTICES OF AVAILABILITY, (8) A RECORD OF DECISION, (9) ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, AND (10) MONTHLY STATUS CALLS.  OPTIONS FOR COMPLETION OF THE EIS UNDER PHASE 3 ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THIS AWARD OF THE DELIVERY ORDER, BUT MAY BE ADDED VIA MODIFICATION TO THIS DELIVERY ORDER OR A SEPARATE DELIVERY ORDER AT A LATER DATE.

description: THIS EFFORT IS FOR THE INVESTIGATION AND DELINEATION OF TEN FORMER WASTE MANAGEMENT AREAS (WMAS), EACH AREA HAS ITS OWN SCOPE OF WORK.

CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001 BAH 10-15 MIAMI ARSR WETLAND RESTORATION THIS IS A TYPE 1 TASK 1. THE 305 ACRE PARCEL OF PROPERTY WAS OUT-LEASED TO JOSE POLO ON JULY 26, 2001 FOR THE PURPOSE OF CATTLE GRAZING. REGARDING AGRICULTURAL ACTIVITIES, THE LEASE STATES, THE LESSEE SHALL NOT UTILIZE THIS PROPERTY FOR ANY AGRICULTURAL PURPOSE THAT WILL BE SUBSIDIZED BY THE GOVERNMENT. 2. SEPTEMBER 2, 2009, MIAMI INTERNATIONAL SSC MANAGER (WILLIAM PINDER) RECEIVED A NOTICE OF VIOLATION FROM DERM. IT STATED THAT DURING AN INSPECTION OF THE SITE ON AUGUST 5, 2009, IT WAS REVEALED THAT WETLANDS HAD BEEN CLEARED AND FILLED, AND CONTINUE TO BE IMPACTED BY VARIOUS AGRICULTURAL OPERATIONS THAT ARE ACTIVELY BEING CONDUCTED ON THE PROPERTY, WITHOUT THE REQUIRED CLASS IV WETLAND PERMIT. 3. ON SEPTEMBER 21 23, 2009, JEREMY HOGARD OF BAH WAS AT THE QM8 SITE TO PERFORM SAMPLING ON THE DEBRIS PILES WHERE WE SUSPECTED HAD PCB CONTAMINATION. IT HAS BEEN DETERMINED THAT THE DEBRIS PILES ARE NOT CONTAMINATED WITH ELEVATED PCB LEVELS. HE PERFORMED SOME RECONNAISSANCE ON THE WETLAND DISTURBANCE SITE. HIS INITIAL REPORT WAS: WETLAND DISTURBANCE EVALUATION: - THE AREA IMPACTED BY FARMLAND IS LOCATED SOUTH AND WEST OF THE QM8-ARSR ACCESS ROAD. - THE AREA CONTAINS MULCH PILES (CONTAINING SHREDDED PIECES OF PLASTIC, CLOTH, CUT WOOD, METAL, ETC.), BANANA PLANTS, PAPAYA PLANTS, OKRA PLANTS, SUGARCANE PLANTS (COULD NOT CONFIRM), AN UNKNOWN STRIP OF PLANTS, AND LARGE AREAS THAT HAVE BEEN PLANTED IN THE PAST (CURRENTLY CONTAIN NATIVE GRASSES). - EACH STRIP OF BANANA AND PAPAYA PLANTS (PLANTED TOGETHER) HAS LARGE ROWS OF MULCH BENEATH THE PLANTS. - THERE IS AN UN-LABELED 55-GALLON DRUM (RUSTED WITH STAINING) IN THE AREA. ALSO, THERE IS A 5-GALLON BUCKET OF "330 TRACTOR FLUID" (A PETROLEUM BASED PRODUCT) IN THE AREA. - THERE WERE SEVERAL ACCESS ROADS LOCATED THROUGH-OUT THE -REA BUT NOT ALL WERE ACCESSIBLE DUE TO MUD AND VEGETATION. 4. ON OCTOBER 16, 2009, THE FAA ISSUED A TERMINATION OF THE OUT-LEASE FOR FARMING/GRAZING PRIVILEGES ON THE PROPERTY. THE FARMER WAS ORDERED TO CEASE ALL GRAZING OPERATIONS AND HAVE HIS CATTLE REMOVED FROM THE PREMISES. 5. ON OCTOBER 28, 2009, JEREMY HOGARD, RYAN ALMASY AND ALLISON HULBERT MET WITH LAZARO QUINTINO OF DERM TO INSPECT THE SITE WITH HIM. BASED ON THIS MEETING, THE FOLLOWING APPROACH HAS BEEN DESIGNED. THE FAA, A FEDERAL GOVERNMENT AGENCY, THE OWNER OF THE LAND, THE STEWARD OF PUBLIC TRUST, AND THE GRANTOR OF RIGHTS TO GRAZE, MUST TAKE THE LEAD ON THIS ISSUE AND BE PREPARED WITH RESOURCES AND EXPERTISE. BASED ON GUIDANCE FROM DERM, A FORMAL WETLAND RESTORATION PLAN WILL BE CONDUCTED. CONTRACT THE DEVELOPMENT OF A RESTORATION PLAN WITH A LOCAL ENVIRONMENTAL CONSULTANT HAVING SUCCESSFUL PAST EXPERIENCE WITHDERM AND WETLAND RESTORATION. CONDUCT RESTORATION AND MONITORING. DEVELOP NATURAL RESOURCE PROTECTION PLAN FOR THE SITE. SCOPE OF WORK: PROJECT MANAGEMENT: - OVERSIGHT OF PROJECT, CONTRACT MANAGEMENT, CLIENT MEETINGS, STATUS REPORTS, ETC IDENTIFY PROFESSIONAL COMPANIES TO MAP AND ASSESS VOLUME OF MULCH AND VEGETATION: - SOLICIT BIDS FROM PROFESSIONAL COMPANIES TO VISIT THE PROPERTY TO QUANTIFY THE VOLUME OF MULCH AND PLANTS WITHIN THE 80 ACRES (+/-). THIS WILL INCLUDE TWO SITE VISITS. THE FIRST WILL BE A TWO-DAY VISIT TO ALLOW ACCESS TO THE QM8-ARSR PROPERTY AND ANSWER QUESTIONS FOR THE COMPANIES BIDDING ON THE MAPPING AND ASSESSMENT ACTIVITIES. THE SECOND VISIT WILL OCCUR, OVER A ONE WEEK PERIOD (5 DAYS), TO PROVIDE OVERSIGHT TO THE COMPANY SELECTED TO CONDUCT THE MAPPING AND ASSESSMENT ACTIVITIES. THE RESULTS OF THE SITE VISITS WILL BE CONVEYED TO FAA AND DERM AS REQUESTED. CONDUCT ADDITIONAL ALTERNATIVE'S RESEARCH FOR RESTORATION OPTIONS OF WETLAND: - COMMUNICATE WITH FAA AND SUBCONTRACTORS TO REVIEW AND DISCUSS OPTIONS AND FEASIBILITY OF ADDITIONAL ALTERNATIVES. SUBMIT ALTERNATIVES TO FAA FOR REVIEW AND CONCURRENCE. COMMUNICATE WITH DERM ABOUT THE ALTERNATIVE(S) SELECTED TO O TAS::69 8107::TAS

CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001 BAH 10-12 ZMA ENV EVALUATION AND CLEANUP TYPE 1 TASK ON AUGUST 6, 2009, A SMALL SPILL OF DIESEL FUEL (APPROXIMATELY EIGHT GALLONS) FROM A DAY TANK OCCURRED ON THE ASPHALT PARKING LOT BEHIND THE ENGINE GENERATOR BUILDING. FAA CONTRACTORS IMMEDIATELY CONTAINED AND CLEANED UP THE DIESEL FUEL SPILL BEFORE IT REACHED THE STORMWATER OUTFALL. HOWEVER, IN AN EFFORT TO CONSERVATIVELY ENSURE NO DIESEL FUEL WAS RELEASED TO SOIL VIA STORMWATER RUNOFF, FAA PERSONNEL COLLECTED FOUR SURFACE SOIL SAMPLES FROM THE GRASSY AREA SOUTH OF THE STORMWATER OUTFALL. THE DEPTH OF THESE SURFACE SOIL SAMPLES WAS FROM 0 TO 6 INCHES BELOW GROUND SURFACE (BGS). THREE OF THE FOUR SURFACE SOIL SAMPLES WERE ANALYZED FOR VOCS, TPHDRO, AND PAHS. ANALYTICAL RESULTS FROM THE SURFACE SOIL SAMPLES WERE COMPARED WITH THE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL. BASED ON THE COMPARISON, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT PAHS AS BENZO(A)PYRENE EQUIVALENTS EXCEEDED THE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL. TWO OTHER PAHS, FLUORANTHENE AND PYRENE, WERE ALSO FOUND TO EXCEED THEIR RESPECTIVE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL. OTHER SVOCS WERE DETECTED, BUT WERE BELOW THE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL. SOIL SAMPLES WERE ALSO ANALYZED FOR VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS (VOCS) AND FLORIDA PETROLEUM RESIDUAL ORGANICS (FL PRO). CONCENTRATIONS OF VOCS WERE ALL BELOW DETECTION LIMITS, WHICH SUGGESTS THAT THE DETECTED SOIL CONTAMINATION IS UNLIKELY TO BE RELATED TO THE AUGUST 2009 SPILL OF DIESEL FUEL. CONCENTRATIONS OF FL PRO WERE BELOW THE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL BUT EXCEEDED CTLS FOR RESIDENTIAL SOIL.  WHILE GROUNDWATER MONITORING HAS BEEN PERFORMED AT OTHER LOCATIONS AT THE ZMA ARTCC SITE, GROUNDWATER HAS NOT BEEN INVESTIGATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE STORMWATER OUTFALL WHERE PAHS WERE DETECTED IN SOIL AT CONCENTRATIONS ABOVE THE FDEP CTLS FOR INDUSTRIAL SOIL. SCOPE OF WORK: PHASE 1 PROJECT MANAGEMENT - OVERSIGHT OF PROJECT, CONTRACT MANAGEMENT, CLIENT MEETINGS, STATUS REPORTS, ETC PHASE 2 CONTINUATION OF SI TO DELINEATE EXTENT OF PAH CONTAMINATION - PREPARE A FOLLOW-UP SAP DOCUMENT TO ADDRESS SECOND SAMPLING EVENT AT THE ZMA-ARTCC; PROVIDE SAP TO FAA FOR REVIEW; ADDRESS COMMENTS TO SAP IMPLEMENTATION OF SI AND FOLLOW-UP SAP - COORDINATE WITH FAA, SUBCONTRACTORS, ETC TO ARRANGE FIELD ACTIVITIES; COORDINATE FOR ANALYSIS OF SAMPLES, ACCESS TO SITE, PLAN SITE VISIT; PREPARE HEALTH AND SAFETY PLANS; CONDUCT SAMPLING VISIT AND COLLECT SAMPLES PREPARE SAR FOR FDEP - OBTAIN ANALYTICAL DATA FROM LAB; OBTAIN DATA FROM SUBCONTRACTORS; ANALYZE DATA; WRITE DRAFT SAR REPORT; COMMUNICATE FINDINGS TO FAA; REVISE SAR REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM FAA; SUBMIT DRAFT SAR REPORT TO REGULATORS; REVISE DRAFT SAR REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM REGULATORS; SUBMIT FINAL SAR REPORT TO REGULATORS; DEVELOP DECISION DOCUMENT PREPARE RI REPORT - OBTAIN ANALYTICAL DATA FROM LAB; OBTAIN DATA FROM SUBCONTRACTORS; ANALYZE DATA; WRITE DRAFT RI REPORT; COMMUNICATE FINDINGS TO FAA; REVISE RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM FAA; SUBMIT DRAFT RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; REVISE DRAFT RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM REGULATORS; SUBMIT FINAL RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; DEVELOP DECISION DOCUMENT. PHASE 3 DEVELOP REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN - EVALUATE REMEDIAL ACTIVITIES METHODOLOGIES; DEVELOP A DRAFT REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN (RAP) THAT INCLUDES COST PROJECTIONS FOR DIFFERENT REMEDIAL ALTERNATIVES; SUBMIT RAP TO FAA FOR COMMENTS; REVISE DRAFT RAP BASED ON FAA COMMENTS; SUBMIT RAP TO FDEP; REVISE DRAFT RAP BASED ON COMMENTS FROM FDEP; SUBMIT FINAL RAP PHASE 4 STAFFING, LOE, ODCS, AND ASSUMPTIONS FOR PHASE 3, 4, 5, AND 6 ACTIVITIES CANNOT BE DETERMINED UNTIL SITE CONTAMINATION HAS BEEN ADEQUATELY DELINEATED AND A REMEDIAL ACTION ALTERNATIVE CAN BE SELECTED. THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE (NPS) IS IN THE PROCESS OF PLANNING FOR REPAIRS AND STABILIZATION OF PORTIONS OF THE YORK RIVER SHORELINE THAT HAVE BEEN SEVERELY ERODING AND THREATENING THE STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY OF THE COLONIAL PARKWAY ROADWAY PRISM. SIGNIFICANT AREAS OF SHORELINE EROSION HAVE AFFECTED THE YORKTOWN BATTLEFIELD, IN ADDITION TO THE COLONIAL PARKWAY.   THE SCOPE OF WORK (SOW) HAS BEEN BROKEN OUT INTO THREE PARTS FOR THIS PARTICULAR PROJECT.  THE FIRST SOW DIRECTS THE CONTRACTOR TO PREPARE AN ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT (EA) TO ASSESS THE IMPACTS OF POTENTIAL ALTERNATIVES FOR THIS WORK.  THE WORK INCLUDES PREPARING THE APPROPRIATE DRAFT DECISION DOCUMENT (FINDING OF NO SIGNIFICANT IMPACT [FONSI] OR NOTICE OF INTENT [NOI]). THE SECOND SOW INCLUDES PREPARING A FEDERAL COASTAL CONSISTENCY DETERMINATION, PREPARING A WETLAND DELINEATION WITHIN THE AREA OF POTENTIAL EFFECT, AND THE THIRD SOW IS FOR PREPARING A WETLAND STATEMENT OF FINDINGS AND A FLOODPLAIN STATEMENT OF FINDINGS (SOF), DOCUMENTING COMPLIANCE WITH THE NPS DIRECTOR'S ORDER AND PROCEDURAL MANUAL #77-1 AND NPS EXECUTIVE ORDER 11988. THE WETLAND SOF AND THE FLOODPLAIN SOF MAY BE COMBINED INTO ONE WETLAND/FLOODPLAIN SOF, AS LONG AS THE REQUIREMENTS FOR BOTH DOCUMENTS ARE MET.  ALTHOUGH THERE ARE THREE SOWS, THE CONTRACTOR SHOULD SUBMIT ONLY ONE PRICE PROPOSAL.

THE FINDINGS FROM THE STUDY WILL ASSIST THE FOREST IN PERFORMING HAZARDOUS MATERIALS ABATEMENT, MATERIALS RECYCLING AND COMPLETE DEMOLITION AND RESTORATION WORK OF THE FORMER BASE IN ORDER TO RETURN THE LAND TO A NATURAL SETTING. WORK ALSO INCLUDES TEST AND NECESSARY RESEARCH TO DETERMINE THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF ANY CONTAMINATION PRESENT AS A RESULT OF THE USE OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS WHEN THE BASE WAS ACTIVE. THE FINDINGS WILL IDENTIFY HAZMAT AND HAZMAT BUILDING COMPONENTS NEEDING TO BE REMOVED PRIOR TO DEMOLITION AND WHETHER SOIL SURROUNDING THE STRUCTURES IS CONTAMINATED AS A RESULT OF OLD STORAGE FACILITIES FOR FUEL, LEAD BASED GASOLINE, ASBESTOS, ETC A DEMOLITION PLAN AND FULL SITE INVENTORY AND CHARACTERIZATION SHALL BE PRODUCED FROM THE INVESTIGATION.

CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001 THIS IS A TYPE 1 TASK  BAH 10-11 MMU ATCT ENV ASSESSMENT AND CLEANUP SCOPE OF WORK: THE SITE IS LOCATED AT THE MORRISTOWN MUNICIPAL AIRPORT (MMU) AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TOWER (ATCT) AT 4 AIRPORT ROAD, MORRISTOWN, MORRIS COUNTY, NEW JERSEY. THE PROPERTY IS OWNED BY THE MUNICIPALITY OF MORRISTOWN. THIS SITE CONSISTS OF THE FORMER LOCATION OF A 550-GALLON GASOLINE UST THAT SERVICED AN EMERGENCY GENERATOR DESIGNED TO PROVIDE BACKUP ELECTRICAL POWER FOR THE ATCT DURING INCIDENTAL POWER OUTAGES. PHASE 1 PROJECT MANAGEMENT - OVERSIGHT OF PROJECT, CONTRACT MANAGEMENT, CLIENT MEETINGS, STATUS REPORTS, ETC. PHASE 2 PREPARE PHASE II INDOOR AIR SAP - PREPARE A SAP DOCUMENT TO ADDRESS INDOOR AIR AND SOIL GAS SAMPLING AT THE MMU ATCT; PROVIDE SAP TO FAA FOR REVIEW; ADDRESS COMMENTS TO SAP; COORDINATE WITH NJDEP TO OBTAIN APPROVAL PHASE II INDOOR AIR SAP IMPLEMENTATION - COORDINATE WITH FAA, SUBCONTRACTORS, ETC TO ARRANGE FIELD ACTIVITIES; COORDINATE FOR ANALYSIS OF SAMPLES, ACCESS TO SITE, PLAN SITE VISIT; PREPARE HEALTH AND SAFETY PLANS; CONDUCT SAMPLING VISIT AND COLLECT SAMPLES. PREPARE PHASE II INDOOR AIR RI REPORT - OBTAIN ANALYTICAL DATA FROM LAB; OBTAIN DATA FROM SUBCONTRACTORS; ANALYZE DATA; WRITE DRAFT RI REPORT; COMMUNICATE FINDINGS TO FAA; REVISE RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM FAA; SUBMIT DRAFT RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; REVISE DRAFT RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM REGULATORS; SUBMIT FINAL RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; DEVELOP DECISION DOCUMENT STAFFING, LOE, ODCS, AND ASSUMPTIONS FOR THESE ACTIVITIES CANNOT BE DETERMINED UNTIL SITE CONTAMINATION HAS BEEN ADEQUATELY DELINEATED AND A REMEDIAL ACTION ALTERNATIVE CAN BE SELECTED. PHASE 3 PREPARE PHASE III INVESTIGATION SAP - PREPARE A SAP DOCUMENT TO FURTHER DELINEATE SOIL AND GROUNDWATER CONTAMINATION AT THE MMU ATCT; PROVIDE SAP TO FAA FOR REVIEW; ADDRESS COMMENTS TO SAP; COORDINATE WITH NJDEP TO OBTAIN APPROVAL PHASE III INVESTIGATION SAP IMPLEMENTATION - COORDINATE WITH FAA, SUBCONTRACTORS, ETC TO ARRANGE FIELD ACTIVITIES; COORDINATE FOR ANALYSIS OF SAMPLES, ACCESS TO SITE, PLAN SITE VISIT; PREPARE HEALTH AND SAFETY PLANS; CONDUCT SAMPLING VISIT AND COLLECT SAMPLES PREPARE PHASE III RI REPORT - OBTAIN ANALYTICAL DATA FROM LAB; OBTAIN DATA FROM SUBCONTRACTORS; ANALYZE DATA; WRITE DRAFT RI REPORT; COMMUNICATE FINDINGS TO FAA; REVISE RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM FAA; SUBMIT DRAFT RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; REVISE DRAFT RI REPORT BASED ON COMMENTS FROM REGULATORS; SUBMIT FINAL RI REPORT TO REGULATORS; DEVELOP DECISION DOCUMENT. PHASE 4 DEVELOP REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN - EVALUATE REMEDIAL ACTIVITIES METHODOLOGIES; DEVELOP A DRAFT REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN (RAP) THAT INCLUDES COST PROJECTIONS FOR DIFFERENT REMEDIAL ALTERNATIVES; SUBMIT RAP TO FAA FOR COMMENTS; REVISE DRAFT RAP BASED ON FAA COMMENTS; SUBMIT RAP TO NJDEP; REVISE DRAFT RAP BASED ON COMMENTS FROM NJDEP; SUBMIT FINAL RAP REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN IMPLEMENTATION - COORDINATE WITH FAA, SUBCONTRACTORS, ETC. TO ARRANGE FIELD ACTIVITIES TO IMPLEMENT RAP; ASSESS THE NEED FOR AND PLAN THE LONG-TERM MONITORING NEEDS AND/OR OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE ACTIVITIES; COORDINATE FOR PURCHASE OF NECESSARY ODCS TO SUPPORT INSTALLATION OF SELECTED RA; ARRANGE ACCESS TO SITE; PLAN SITE VISIT; PREPARE HEALTH AND SAFETY PLANS; IMPLEMENT SELECTED RA PHASE 5 REMEDIAL ACTION PLAN OPTIMIZATION/ LONG-TERM MONITORING - ONCE IMPLEMENTED, EVALUATE THE REMEDIAL ACTION TO DETERMINE IF OPTIMIZATION IS NEEDED; IMPLEMENT CHANGES TO THE REMEDIAL ACTION AS NEEDED; COMMUNICATE WITH FAA, SUBCONTRACTORS, AND REGULATORS ABOUT RA STATUS; EVALUATE LONG-TERM MONITORING REQUIREMENTS AND AUGMENT (WHERE NECESSARY) OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE ACTIVITIES; FURTHER DELINEATE CONTAMINATION, IF NECESSARY, WITH ADDITIONAL WELLS AND SOIL SAMPLING. CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001  BAH 10-7  OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY & HEALTH THIS IS A TYPE 1 TASK OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY AND HEALTH (OSH) PROGRAM AREA: HEALTH & SAFETY   PROJECT: FLIGHT STANDARDS OSH PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION.  CONTACT PERSON: ROSS CARROLL 404-474-5765 SCOPE OF WORK MAY INCLUDE THESE ITEMS:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL ASSIST AFS IN UPDATING ITS OSH PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION PLAN. THIS WILL INCLUDE INCREASING THE DETAIL OF THE CURRENT PLAN TO ADDRESS SPECIFIC PROGRAM ELEMENTS TO BE DEVELOPED (OSHA REQUIREMENTS AND ALIGNED INDUSTRY STANDARDS SUCH AS ANSI Z10), THE ACTIVITIES TO BE CONDUCTED, RESOURCES REQUIRED (BOTH STAFF AND BUDGETARY) AND SCHEDULE TO IMPLEMENT THESE ELEMENTS DOWN TO THE WORK PACKAGE LEVEL. THE CONTRACTOR WILL WORK WITH THE AFS OSH PROGRAM MANAGER TO ESTABLISH A NATIONAL STEERING GROUP TO PROVIDE INPUT AND REVIEW OF THE AFS OSH IMPLEMENTATION PLAN TO ENSURE IT IS PRACTICAL AND HAS THE BUY-IN OF KEY STAKEHOLDERS. THE CONTRACTOR WILL FACILITATE A KICK- OFF MEETING AND TWO (2) IN-PERSON WORKING GROUP SESSIONS INCLUDING DEVELOPMENT OF DETAILED MEETING MINUTES AND TRACKING OF ACTION ITEMS. ADDITIONALLY, UP TO SIX (6) TELEPHONIC STATUS MEETINGS SHALL BE HELD WHILE THE IMPLEMENTATION PLAN AND SCHEDULE ARE BEING COMPILED. THE CURRENT AFS OSH IMPLEMENTATION PLAN SHALL BE REVIEWED AGAINST 29 CFR 1910 AND 1960 REQUIREMENTS, CURRENT FAA OSH ORDERS AND GUIDANCE, AND INDUSTRY BEST PRACTICES. THIS REVIEW WILL IDENTIFY ANY AREAS REQUIRING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION OR SECTIONS SIMPLY REQUIRING REVISION WITHIN THE CURRENT IMPLEMENTATION PLAN. AREAS REQUIRING IMPROVEMENT, ALONG WITH RECOMMENDATIONS FOR THE CHANGES TO BE MADE, WILL BE DOCUMENTED IN A FORMAL GAP ANALYSIS REPORT FOR REVIEW AND COMMENT BY THE AFS OSH PROGRAM MANAGER AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS. THE CONCURRED FINDINGS AND RECOMMENDATIONS FROM THE GAP ANALYSIS REPORT WILL BE PRESENTED TO THE NATIONAL STEERING GROUP AT THE AFS IMPLEMENTATION PLAN REVISION KICKOFF MEETING ALONG WITH KEY ELEMENTS OF THE IMPLEMENTATION PLAN SUCH AS: ı PROGRAM SCOPE ı MAJOR OSH PROGRAM AREAS (E.G., RESPIRATORY PROTECTION, PERSONAL PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT [PPE], FALL PROTECTION, LOCKOUT/TAGOUT, HAZCOM) ı MANAGEMENT SYSTEM ELEMENTS SUCH AS THOSE COVERED BY THE OSHSMS ANSI Z10 STANDARD (E.G., POLICIES, PROCEDURES, TRAINING, DOCUMENT CONTROLS, AUDITING, PERFORMANCE REVIEW) USED TO DRIVE CULTURE CHANGE (AND PROGRAM THAT IS NOT RELIANT ON KEY PEOPLE, BUT STRONG PROCESSES) ı PROGRAM PERFORMANCE GOALS, METRICS, AND REPORTING PROCESSES ı STAFFING REQUIREMENTS (E.G., COLLATERAL DUTY SAFETY AND HEALTH (CDSH) PERSONNEL, INSPECTORS, OSH PROFESSIONALS, AS WELL AS CONTRACT SUPPORT); ı TRAINING PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT AND DEPLOYMENT ı MONITORING (E.G., AUDITING/INSPECTION PROGRAMS) ı STRATEGIC COMMUNICATION/INTERNAL MARKETING AND AWARENESS; ı DATA TRACKING, RECORDS MANAGEMENT AND REPORTING (INCLUDING DATABASES THAT MAY BE NECESSARY); ı BUDGETARY RESOURCES AND IMPLEMENTATION SCHEDULE. BASED ON INPUT COLLECTED DURING THE NATIONAL IMPLEMENTATION STEERING GROUP MEETING, A DRAFT REVISION OF THE OSH PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION PLAN SHALL BE PREPARED THAT INCLUDES A GENERAL INTRODUCTORY SECTION WITH BACKGROUND INFORMATION AND A DISCUSSION OF THE SCOPE OF THE PROGRAM. THE DRAFT REVISED PLAN SHALL ALSO PROVIDE DETAILED PLANS, INCLUDING A SCHEDULE OF SPECIFIC ACTIVITIES TO THE TASK (WORK PACKAGE) LEVEL FOR THE IMPLEMENTATION, EXECUTION AND MONITORING OF THE OVERALL AFS OSH PROGRAM AND EACH SUBSIDIARY PROGRAM AT EACH AFS FACILITY. A SCHEDULE FOR THE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF ALL BUSINESS AND DATA MANAGEMENT PROCESSES, POLICIES, GUIDANCE AND ORDERS OR PROCEDURES SHALL ALSO BE ESTABLISHED. SUPPORT FOR THIS PROGRAM MAY INCLUDE, BUT IS NOT LIMITED TO: CONSULTATION, POLICY DEVELOPMENT, REGULATORY ANALYSIS, INVESTIGATIONS, SURVEYS, TRAINING, SAMPLING, MONITORING, NOTICE OF VIOLATION TRACKING, ASSESSMENTS, DESIGN, SPECIFICATION AND DESIGN REVIEWS, AND PROGRAM PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION SERVICES. ı GENERAL CONSULTATIONS - THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES THAT CAN PROVIDE GENERAL TECHNICAL AND CONSULTATION IN SUPPORT FOR THE FST PROGRAM AREA. THIS SUPPORT MUST INCLUDE RESEARCH INTO FEDERAL, STATE, AND LOCAL REGULATIONS, FAA ORDERS, PROFESSIONAL AGENCY REGULATIONS AND STANDARDS, AND DETERMINE HOW THEY APPLY TO THE FAA. THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THESE ACTIONS IS ESSENTIAL TO MAINTAINING PROPER RELIABILITY OF THE FST SYSTEMS IN THE FIELD WHILE SUSTAINING REGULATORY COMPLIANCE. ı SPILL PREVENTION CONTROL, AND COUNTERMEASURES (SPCC) PLANS, TRAINING, AND REVIEWS: THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES TO GENERATE AND MAINTAIN SPCC PLANS AS REQUIRED FST SYSTEMS. THE CONTRACTOR WILL DEVELOP AND TEACH THE REQUIRED SPCC PLAN TRAINING WITH SPCC PLANS. THE SPCC PLAN TRAINING MUST BE FACILITY-SPECIFIC AND INCLUDE, BUT NOT BE LIMITED TO: BASIC FST SYSTEMS, THE FST SYSTEMS AND EQUIPMENT THAT CONTAIN REGULATED LIQUIDS, POTENTIAL SPILL ROUTES AND RECEPTORS, AND SPILL RESPONSE ACTIVITIES AND PROTOCOLS. ADDITIONALLY, THE CONTRACTOR WILL REVIEW EXISTING SPCC PLANS, AS REQUIRED, FOR CORRECTNESS, COMPLETENESS, AND CONFORMANCE WITH PROPER SPCC PLAN PROTOCOLS AS STATED IN EPA 40 CFR, PART 112. THE CONTRACTOR MUST BE CAPABLE OF IDENTIFYING, REPLACING, AND/OR CORRECTING DEFICIENT SPCC PLANS. ı FST SYSTEM COMPLIANCE INSPECTIONS: THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES CAPABLE OF REVIEWING FST SYSTEM DRAWINGS AS WELL AS INSPECTING NEW FST SYSTEM INSTALLATIONS DURING AND AFTER CONSTRUCTION FOR COMPLIANCE WITH FAA, FEDERAL, STATE, AND OTHER REGULATING AGENCY REQUIREMENTS. INSPECTION CAPABILITIES INCLUDE KNOWLEDGE AND UNDERSTANDING OF FST SYSTEM EQUIPMENT AND MATERIALS COMMONLY USED FOR FAA INSTALLATIONS, CURRENT AND NEW CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS AND EQUIPMENT, AND LOCAL REQUIREMENTS BASED ON GENERAL AND SPECIFIC LOCATION, WEATHER, TEMPERATURE, AND REGIONAL/PERSONNEL PREFERENCES. BASED ON THE INSPECTION RESULTS, THE CONTRACTOR WILL PROVIDE ORAL COMMUNICATION AND WRITTEN EVALUATION OF THE INSPECTION RESULTS TO THE APPROPRIATE FAA PERSONNEL. ı FIELD SERVICES (SYSTEM/PROGRAM AUDITS) - THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE FIELD-BASED FST SYSTEM AND PROGRAM AUDITS CONTAINED IN THE ESA AS REQUESTED BY THE FAA. THE CONTRACTOR MUST BE RESPONSIBLE FOR DEVELOPING A WORK PLAN TO DEFINE COMPLIANCE AND RISK MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES OF THE FIELD AUDITS; INCLUDING ESTIMATED COSTS AND SCHEDULES, AND SPECIFIC QUALITY ASSURANCE/QUALITY CONTROL (QA/QC) PROTOCOLS TO ACHIEVE REQUIRED LEVELS OF DATA QUALITY AND DEFENSIBILITY. CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-00001 BAH 10-8 THIS IS A TYPE 2 TASK INDOOR AIR QUALITY SCOPE OF WORK: MAY INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: INDOOR AIR QUALITY. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES TO PERFORM INDOOR AIR QUALITY INVESTIGATIONS. THIS RESOURCE MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WORKING IN THE FAA AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL ENVIRONMENT AND DIPLOMACY TO HANDLE THE FRAGILE FAA ENVIRONMENT WITH REGARDS TO THE BARGAINING UNITS AND MANAGEMENT ISSUES. THIS SUPPORT INCLUDES RESPONDING TO AIR QUALITY COMPLAINTS OR CONCERNS, ASSESSING THE PROBLEM, AND PROVIDING TECHNICAL RECOMMENDATIONS FOR EACH FACILITY BASED ON PRIOR FAA KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE. HEARING CONSERVATION: THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE EXPERTISE IN HEARING CONSERVATION AND NOISE EVALUATIONS FOR THE ESA. THIS EXPERTISE SHALL INCLUDE NOISE SURVEYS AT FACILITIES LOCATED ON AIRPORTS AND IN POTENTIAL HIGH NOISE AREAS SUCH AS ENGINE GENERATOR ROOMS. THIS SUPPORT MAY INCLUDE THE USE OF SOUND LEVEL METERS AND NOISE DOSIMETRY. ERGONOMICS: THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE EXPERTISE TO PERFORM OFFICE AND INDUSTRIAL ERGONOMIC SURVEYS TO IDENTIFY DEFICIENCIES, PROVIDE RECOMMENDATIONS, AND DESIGN CORRECTIVE ACTIONS. THIS SUPPORT SHALL ALSO INCLUDE REVIEW OF DESIGN PACKAGES FOR POTENTIAL ERGONOMIC ISSUES. ASBESTOS AND LEAD. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE EXPERTISE WITH A GOOD UNDERSTANDING OF THE FAA ASBESTOS CONTROL PROGRAM, THE STANDARD PROCEDURES WITHIN THE FAA WITH REGARDS TO ASBESTOS AND LEAD MONITORING, SAMPLING, AND MANAGEMENT. THIS ALSO INCLUDES MAINTAINING EXISTING ASBESTOS AND/OR LEAD DATA MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS MAINTAINED AT THE EASTERN SERVICE CENTER. SUPPORT INCLUDES DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT, MAINTENANCE, AND MANAGEMENT OF ASBESTOS RELATED INFORMATION THAT IS CURRENTLY AVAILABLE ON THE ESA INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE KSN SITE. EQUIPMENT CALIBRATION. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE EXPERTISE TO DEVELOP A SYSTEM THAT ENSURES THAT INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE EQUIPMENT REMAINS IN CALIBRATION BASED ON FAA AND MANUFACTURER S SPECIFICATIONS. THIS PROGRAM SHALL INCLUDE ALL INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE INSTRUMENTATION INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO RADIATION, NOISE, IAQ, PIDS, ROTOMETERS, AND HEAT STRESS METERS. GENERAL INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE SUPPORT/PROGRAM MANAGEMENT. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE RESOURCES FOR GENERAL INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE PROGRAM TECHNICAL SUPPORT WHICH INCLUDES DEVELOPING AND/OR FACILITATING BRIEFINGS ON ISSUES TO SENIOR FAA MANAGEMENT, PARTICIPATION AND/OR FACILITATION OF MEETINGS, WORKSHOPS, AND CONFERENCES AND ASSOCIATED TRAVEL; REGULATORY REVIEWS AND RECORD CHECKS; PRESENTATION PREPARATION; COST TRACKING; SERVICE CENTER SUPPORT; DEVELOPMENT OF DATABASES AND TRACKING SYSTEMS; PROJECT PLANNING; TECHNICAL WORK PLAN DEVELOPMENT; INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE ADVISORY CONSULTATION SERVICES; SUBCONTRACTOR/VENDOR PROCUREMENT; COORDINATION OF FIELD RELATED ACTIVITIES; AND REPORT GENERATION. GENERAL CONSULTATIONS - THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES FOR GENERAL TECHNICAL AND CONSULTATION SUPPORT FOR THE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE PROGRAM AREA. THIS SUPPORT MUST INCLUDE RESEARCH INTO REGULATIONS, FAA ORDERS, CONSENSUS STANDARDS, AND INTERPRETING HOW THEY APPLY TO THE FAA. UNDERSTANDING OF THE FAA S INFRASTRUCTURE AND THE NATURE OF HOW MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTS SPECIFIC ACTIONS IN THE FIELD IS ESSENTIAL FOR AN EFFECTIVE PROGRAM. ACCIDENT INVESTIGATIONS AND ISSUE RESOLUTION. THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES TO ADDRESS THE INDUSTRIAL HYGIENE ISSUES THAT REQUIRE A CIH TO ASSESS AND PROVIDE RECOMMENDATIONS DURING ACCIDENT INVESTIGATIONS. THIS IS VERY EVIDENT DURING ACCIDENT INVESTIGATIONS WHERE A CIH IS REQUIRED TO HAVE KNOWLEDGE OF THE LABOR RELATIONS REGULATIONS, THE MANAGEMENT STRUCTURE WITHIN THE FAA, AND HOW THE PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS ARE IMPLEMENTED IN THE FIELD IN ORDER TO PERFORM A THOROUGH INVESTIGATION AND DEVELOP A REPORT WITH FUNCTIONAL RECOMMENDATIONS. JUSTIFICATION: CT DEEP IS WORKING TOWARDS THE DEVELOPMENT OF A STATEWIDE TMDL ADDRESSING NUTRIENT LOADING AND HAB FORMATION WITH WATERSHED SPECIFIC APPENDICES TO ADDRESS SITE-SPECIFIC CONDITIONS. CT DEEP HAS SUCCESSFULLY USED THIS TMDL FORMAT (A CORE DOCUMENT WITH WATERSHED-SPECIFIC APPENDICES) PREVIOUSLY TO ADDRESS RECREATIONAL AND SHELL FISHING IMPAIRMENTS DUE TO BACTERIA AS WELL AS TO ADDRESS AQUATIC LIFE IMPACTS FROM STORM WATER. ADDRESSING NUTRIENT IMPAIRMENT AND HABS IS A PRIORITY FOR EPA. FUNDING CT'S TMDL WILL ALLOW THE STATE TO ADDRESS ALL THE LAKE AND IMPOUNDMENT NUTRIENT-IMPAIRED WATERS LISTINGS IS A GREATLY EXPEDITED MANNER.  THIS FUNDING IS NEEDED TO EVALUATE NUTRIENT LOADING AND ESTIMATE WATERSHED BASED ANNUAL LOAD REDUCTIONS FOR BANTAM LAKE. BANTAM LAKE WILL BE THE FIRST APPENDIX THAT WILL ACCOMPANY THE STATEWIDE NUTRIENT TMDL. THE FUNDING WILL BE USED TO PERFORM MODELING OF THE LAKE USING THE BATHTUB MODEL AND TRAIN CT DEEP STAFF TO USE THE MODEL TO COMPLETE THE REMAINDER OF THE STATE'S NUTRIENT-IMPAIRED LAKES AND IMPOUNDMENTS.   THERE IS SIGNIFICANT FEDERAL INTEREST IN ENSURING THAT THE NUTRIENT-IMPAIRED WATER BODIES LISTED ON THE CT IMPAIRED WATERS LIST ARE ADDRESSED BY TMDLS IN THE MOST EXPEDITIOUS TIME PERIOD POSSIBLE. THIS STATE-WIDE TMDL WILL SERVE AS A MODEL NATIONALLY FOR AN EFFICIENT APPROACH TO ADDRESSING NUTRIENT IMPAIRMENTS FOR LAKES AND IMPOUNDMENTS.

CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001 BAH-10-3 THIS IS A TYPE 2 TASK. ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE SCOPE OF WORK PROGRAM PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION SERVICES - ASSIST THE FAA IN THE PLANNING, DEVELOPMENT, AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THEIR ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE PROGRAM, INCLUDING DETERMINING THE EXTENT OF THE PROGRAM, IDENTIFY PROGRAM GOALS AND OBJECTIVES, DEVELOP PROGRAM OPTIONS AND IMPLEMENTATION PLANS, PRIORITIZE ASSOCIATED TASKS, EVALUATE AND OBTAIN SUBCONTRACT SUPPORT TO PERFORM SPECIFIC TASKS, PROVIDE TECHNICAL OVERSIGHT OF THESE EFFORTS, MANAGE THE DATA COLLECTED DURING THESE EFFORTS, AND UPDATE DATABASES AS REQUIRED. CONSULTATIONS PROVIDE GENERAL TECHNICAL CONSULTATION IN SUPPORT OF THE ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAM AREAS. THIS INCLUDES RESEARCH OF ASSIGNED ISSUES, DEVELOPMENT OF RECOMMENDATIONS, AND DELIVERY OF OPTION PACKAGES FOR USE BY THE FAA TO SUPPORT ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE AND PERFORMANCE NEEDS. POLICY DEVELOPMENT ASSIST THE FAA WITH THE DEVELOPMENT OF SERVICE AREA POLICY AND/OR GUIDANCE DOCUMENTS. THESE RESOURCES MUST RESEARCH ASSIGNED ISSUES, COLLECT AND ANALYZE DATA, AND DEVELOP POLICY RECOMMENDATIONS AND/OR ALTERNATIVES BASED ON COSTS, BENEFITS, TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY, AND OPERATIONAL IMPACTS. ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE PROGRAM (ECP) ASSIST THE FAA WITH UPDATING AND MAINTAINING THE STATUS OF THE ACTION ITEMS IN THE ECP AND ASSISTING WITH THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE ACTION ITEMS AS REQUESTED. SPECIFIC TASKING MAY INCLUDE: GENERAL PLANNING SUPPORT; DEVELOPMENT OF AN ECP INFORMATION MANAGEMENT APPLICATION; AND ANY OTHER ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE RELATED POLICY DEVELOPMENT INITIATIVE IDENTIFIED BY THE FAA. NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACT (NEPA) - PROVIDE SUPPORT TO THE FAA S NEPA PROGRAM AND TO ASSIST FAA IN EVALUATING AND MINIMIZING THE EFFECT OF FAA/FEDERAL ACTIONS ON THE ENVIRONMENT. SPECIFIC SUPPORT MUST INCLUDE DEVELOPING AND PROVIDING INITIAL TRAINING, ESTABLISHING NEPA POLICY AND PROCEDURES FOR DISTRICT PROJECTS; PREPARING AND/OR REVIEWING NEPA DOCUMENTATION, AND WORKING WITH ENGINEERING SERVICES TO DEVELOP A SYSTEM TO TRACK NEPA MITIGATION MEASURES. ECOLOGICAL AND CULTURAL RESOURCE SUPPORT FINALIZING PROGRAMMATIC AGREEMENTS AT EACH OF THE STATE HISTORIC PRESERVATION OFFICES (SHPO), DEVELOPING AND/OR REVIEWING CULTURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT PLANS; DEVELOPING OR REVIEWING REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENTS; DEVELOPING OR REVIEWING GIS MANAGEMENT APPLICATIONS; CONSULTATION UNDER SECTION 106 OF THE HISTORIC PRESERVATION ACT TO AVOID IMPACTS TO RESOURCES ELIGIBLE OR LISTED ON THE NATIONAL REGISTER OF HISTORIC PLACES; AND COORDINATING WITH RESOURCE AGENCIES TO DEVELOP MEASURES TO MINIMIZE HARM WHERE IMPACTS CANNOT BE AVOIDED. REVISE THE ECOLOGICAL AND CULTURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT PLAN DRINKING WATER: PROVIDE FACILITY EVALUATIONS FOR DRINKING WATER SYSTEMS. SUPPORT INCLUDES EVALUATION OF DRINKING WATER LABORATORY RESULTS AND PROVIDING RECOMMENDATIONS TO REDUCE CONTAMINATES AND CORROSION WITHIN THE FACILITY WATER SUPPLY SYSTEM. ADDITIONAL SUPPORT INCLUDES WATER SYSTEM ANALYSIS, REMEDIATION RECOMMENDATIONS, AND COST PLANNING. AIR PERMITTING: PROVIDE AIR PERMITTING ASSISTANCE TO THE FAA. THIS INCLUDES AN UNDERSTANDING OF THE FAA S INVENTORY OF AIR POLLUTION EMITTING SOURCES, SUCH AS GENERATORS AND BOILERS. RESOURCES PROVIDE RECOMMENDATIONS AND PERMIT DETERMINATIONS FOR FACILITIES LOCATED IN ALL STATES AND TERRITORIES ENCOMPASSED WITHIN FAA S EASTERN SERVICE AREA. SEMI-ANNUAL EVALUATIONS USING EPA S GREEN BOOK ARE NECESSARY TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH NON-ATTAINMENT STATUES FOR EACH STATE, AS IT RELATES TO FAA S AIR EMISSION SOURCES. TRAINING: DEVELOP AND PROVIDE TRAINING RELATED TO THE TECHNICAL, REGULATORY, STRATEGIC, AND PROGRAM MANAGEMENT COMPONENTS OF THE ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE PROGRAMS. ON OCCASION, THESE RESOURCES MUST DELIVER PRESENTATIONS DURING ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE PROGRAM/TEAM MEETINGS. CONTRACT # DTFASO-10-D-00001  BAH-10-4 ENVIRONMENTAL CLEANUP  THIS IS A TYPE 2 TASK.  SCOPE OF WORK:    CLEANUP PROGRAM: THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES TO ASSIST THE FAA IN THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE FAA'S ENVIRONMENTAL CLEAN-UP PROGRAM (ECU). THE RESOURCES ASSIST WITH THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE ANNUAL UPDATES FOR ESA-ESCR; ASSIST WITH ASSESSMENT AND IDENTIFICATION OF ALL FACILITIES WITH POTENTIAL ENVIRONMENTAL/CLEAN-UP ISSUES, IDENTIFY RELEVANT ENVIRONMENTAL CLEANUP ISSUES AFFECTING FAA FACILITIES AND SOPS FOR EVALUATING AND COMPLETING THE CHECKLIST AND SITE/FACILITY ASSESSMENTS; SPECIFIC  SERVICES AND ACTIVITIES MUST INCLUDE IDENTIFYING PROGRAM REPORTING RELATIONSHIPS AND RESPONSIBILITIES; STAFFING REQUIREMENTS AND RESOURCES; DEVELOPING REGIONAL CLEAN-UP POLICIES, OPERATIONAL PLANS, AND PROGRAMS; AND IDENTIFYING PERSONNEL AND FINANCIAL RESOURCES FOR PROGRAM EXECUTION. SUPPORT INCLUDES ASSISTING FAA IN ENSURING PROCESSES, EQUIPMENT, AND CONTRACTS ARE IN PLACE TO FULFILL  TECHNICAL REGULATORY STANDARDS AS WELL AS FAA POLICIES AND LABOR AGREEMENTS. THE CONTRACTOR MUST ASSIST THE FAA IN PERFORMING PERIODIC REVIEWS AND ASSESSMENTS TO MONITOR CLEAN-UP PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS; PROVIDE GENERAL MANAGEMENT OF ECU PROGRAM FOR ESA; COORDINATE AND ATTEND MEETINGS WITH CLIENTS TO DISCUSS TASK STATUS; AND SUPPORT NON-ROUTINE TASKS THAT INCLUDE CLIENT INITIATED  ENVIRONMENTAL QUESTIONS, RESEARCH, MEETING SUPPORT, AND GUIDANCE. SPECIFIC ACTIVITIES MUST INCLUDE DEVELOPING A CLEAN-UP INSPECTION PROCESS AND PERFORMING CLEAN-UP PROGRAM SELF-ASSESSMENTS.  NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACT (NEPA) - THE CONTRACTOR MUST PROVIDE EXPERT RESOURCES TO PROVIDE SUPPORT TO THE FAA'S NEPA PROGRAM AS IT RELATES TO THE ECU PROGRAM; ASSIST FAA IN EVALUATING AND MINIMIZING THE EFFECT OF FAA/FEDERAL ACTIONS ON THE ENVIRONMENT DURING THE CONDUCT OF CLEANUP ACTIONS.
